elements.name: 氢
elements.summary: 氢是一种化学元素，化学符号为 H，原子序数为 1。氢的原子量为 1.00794 u，是周期表中最轻的元素。其单原子形式（H）是宇宙中含量最丰富的化学物质，约占所有重子质量的 75%。

image.title: 装有发光的超纯氢气（H2）的小瓶。原始尺寸（厘米）：1 x 5
image.attribution: User:Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/hydrogen.php

elements.summary_en: Hydrogen is a chemical element with chemical symbol H and atomic number 1. With an atomic weight of 1.00794 u, hydrogen is the lightest element on the periodic table. Its monatomic form (H) is the most abundant chemical substance in the Universe, constituting roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

elements.name: 氦
elements.appearance: 无色气体，置于高压电场中时呈现红橙色光泽
elements.summary: 氦是一种化学元素，符号为 He，原子序数为 2。它是一种无色、无嗅、无味、无毒、惰性的单原子气体，在周期表中位列稀有气体组之首。它的沸点和熔点在所有元素中是最低的。

image.title: 装有发光的超纯氦气的小瓶。原始尺寸（厘米）：1 x 5
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/helium.php

elements.summary_en: Helium is a chemical element with symbol He and atomic number 2. It is a colorless, odorless, tasteless, non-toxic, inert, monatomic gas that heads the noble gas group in the periodic table. Its boiling and melting points are the lowest among all the elements.
elements.appearance_en: colorless gas, exhibiting a red-orange glow when placed in a high-voltage electric field

elements.name: 锂
elements.discovered_by: 约翰·奥古斯特·阿夫韦松
elements.summary: 锂（源自希腊语：λίθος lithos，“石头”）是一种化学元素，符号为 Li，原子序数为 3。它是一种柔软的银白色金属，属于碱金属组。在标准条件下，它是最轻的金属和密度最小的固体元素。

image.title: 氩气保护下的 0.5 克锂。最大块的原始尺寸（厘米）：0.3 x 4
image.attribution: Hi-Res Images ofChemical Elements, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/lithium.php

elements.summary_en: Lithium (from Greek:λίθος lithos, "stone") is a chemical element with the symbol Li and atomic number 3. It is a soft, silver-white metal belonging to the alkali metal group of chemical elements. Under standard conditions it is the lightest metal and the least dense solid element.

elements.name: 铍
elements.summary: 铍是一种化学元素，符号为 Be，原子序数为 4。它通过恒星核合成产生，在宇宙中是相对稀有的元素。铍是一种二价元素，在自然界中仅以与其他元素结合形成矿物的形式存在。

image.title: 纯铍珠，2.5 克。原始尺寸（厘米）：1 x 1.5
image.attribution: Hi-Res Images ofChemical Elements, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/beryllium.php

elements.summary_en: Beryllium is a chemical element with symbol Be and atomic number 4. It is created through stellar nucleosynthesis and is a relatively rare element in the universe. It is a divalent element which occurs naturally only in combination with other elements in minerals.

elements.name: 硼
elements.summary: 硼是一种类金属化学元素，符号为 B，原子序数为 5。硼完全由宇宙射线散裂和超新星产生，而非恒星核合成，因此在太阳系和地壳中都是低丰度元素。在地球上，硼由于其常见的天然化合物（硼酸盐矿物）具有水溶性而得以富集。

image.title: 纯结晶硼，正面和背面。原始尺寸（厘米）：2 x 3
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/boron.php

elements.summary_en: Boron is a metalloid chemical element with symbol B and atomic number 5. Produced entirely by cosmic ray spallation and supernovae and not by stellar nucleosynthesis, it is a low-abundance element in both the Solar system and the Earth's crust. Boron is concentrated on Earth by the water-solubility of its more common naturally occurring compounds, the borate minerals.

elements.name: 碳
elements.summary: 碳（源自拉丁语：carbo “煤”）是一种化学元素，符号为 C，原子序数为 6。在周期表中，它是第 14 列（组）六个元素中的第一个（第 2 行），这些元素的共同点是外层电子壳层的组成。它是非金属且为四价——提供四个电子以形成共价化学键。

image.title: 第 6 号元素 - 碳
image.attribution: Texas Lane, CC BY-SA 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Carbon (from Latin:carbo "coal") is a chemical element with symbol C and atomic number 6. On the periodic table, it is the first (row 2) of six elements in column (group) 14, which have in common the composition of their outer electron shell. It is nonmetallic and tetravalent—making four electrons available to form covalent chemical bonds.

elements.name: 氮
elements.summary: 氮是一种化学元素，符号为 N，原子序数为 7。它是最轻的氮族元素，在室温下是一种透明、无嗅的双原子气体。氮是宇宙中常见的元素，估计在银河系和太阳系的总丰度中排名第七左右。

image.title: 装有发光的超纯氮气（N2）的小瓶。原始尺寸（厘米）：1 x 5
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/nitrogen.php

elements.summary_en: Nitrogen is a chemical element with symbol N and atomic number 7. It is the lightest pnictogen and at room temperature, it is a transparent, odorless diatomic gas. Nitrogen is a common element in the universe, estimated at about seventh in total abundance in the Milky Way and the Solar System.

elements.name: 氧
elements.summary: 氧是一种化学元素，符号为 O，原子序数为 8。它是周期表中氧族元素的成员，是一种高度活泼的非金属和氧化剂，极易与大多数元素形成化合物（特别是氧化物）。按质量计算，氧是宇宙中丰度第三高的元素，仅次于氢和氦。

image.title: 烧杯中的液氧
image.attribution: Staff Sgt. Nika Glover, U.S. Air Force, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Oxygen is a chemical element with symbol O and atomic number 8. It is a member of the chalcogen group on the periodic table and is a highly reactive nonmetal and oxidizing agent that readily forms compounds (notably oxides) with most elements. By mass, oxygen is the third-most abundant element in the universe, after hydrogen and helium.

elements.name: 氟
elements.summary: 氟是一种化学元素，符号为 F，原子序数为 9。它是最轻的卤素，在标准条件下以剧毒的淡黄色双原子气体形式存在。作为电负性最强的元素，它极具活性：几乎所有其他元素，包括一些稀有气体，都能与氟形成化合物。

image.title: -196°C 下的液氟
image.attribution: Fulvio314, CC BY-SA 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Fluorine is a chemical element with symbol F and atomic number 9. It is the lightest halogen and exists as a highly toxic pale yellow diatomic gas at standard conditions. As the most electronegative element, it is extremely reactive:almost all other elements, including some noble gases, form compounds with fluorine.

elements.name: 氖
elements.appearance: 无色气体，置于高压电场中时呈现橙红色光泽
elements.summary: 氖是一种化学元素，符号为 Ne，原子序数为 10。它位于周期表的第 18 组（稀有气体）。在标准条件下，氖是一种无色、无嗅、惰性的单原子气体，密度约为空气的三分之二。

image.title: 装有发光的超纯氖气的小瓶。原始尺寸（厘米）：1 x 5
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/neon.php

elements.summary_en: Neon is a chemical element with symbol Ne and atomic number 10. It is in group 18 (noble gases) of the periodic table. Neon is a colorless, odorless, inert monatomic gas under standard conditions, with about two-thirds the density of air.
elements.appearance_en: colorless gas exhibiting an orange-red glow when placed in a high voltage electric field

elements.name: 钠
elements.summary: 钠是一种化学元素，符号为 Na（源自古希腊语 Νάτριο），原子序数为 11。它是一种柔软、银白色、高度活泼的金属。在周期表中，它位于第 1 列（碱金属），与该列的其他六个元素一样，它的外壳层只有一个电子，且极易失去该电子，从而产生带正电荷的原子——阳离子。

image.title: 钠（Na）金属
image.attribution: The original uploader was Dnn87 at English Wikipedia., CC BY-SA 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Sodium /ˈsoʊdiəm/ is a chemical element with symbol Na (from Ancient Greek Νάτριο) and atomic number 11. It is a soft, silver-white, highly reactive metal. In the Periodic table it is in column 1 (alkali metals), and shares with the other six elements in that column that it has a single electron in its outer shell, which it readily donates, creating a positively charged atom - a cation.

elements.name: 镁
elements.summary: 镁是一种化学元素，符号为 Mg，原子序数为 12。它是一种闪亮的灰色固体，与周期表第二列（第 2 组，或碱土金属）的其他五个元素在物理性质上非常相似：它们在外层电子壳层具有相同的电子排布，从而产生相似的晶体结构。镁是宇宙中丰度排名第九的元素。

image.title: 镁晶体
image.attribution: Warut Roonguthai, CC BY-SA 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Magnesium is a chemical element with symbol Mg and atomic number 12. It is a shiny gray solid which bears a close physical resemblance to the other five elements in the second column (Group 2, or alkaline earth metals) of the periodic table:they each have the same electron configuration in their outer electron shell producing a similar crystal structure. Magnesium is the ninth most abundant element in the universe.

elements.name: 铝
elements.summary: 铝是一种硼族化学元素，符号为 Al，原子序数为 13。它是一种银白色、柔软、无磁性、具有延展性的金属。铝是地壳中丰度第三高的元素（仅次于氧和硅），也是地壳中含量最丰富的金属。

image.title: 纯铝箔。原始尺寸（厘米）：5 x 5
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/aluminium.php

elements.summary_en: Aluminium (or aluminum; see different endings) is a chemical element in the boron group with symbol Al and atomic number 13. It is a silvery-white, soft, nonmagnetic, ductile metal. Aluminium is the third most abundant element (after oxygen and silicon), and the most abundant metal, in the Earth's crust.

elements.name: 硅
elements.appearance: 结晶状，具有蓝色色调的面，具反光性
elements.discovered_by: 约恩斯·雅各布·贝采利乌斯
elements.summary: 硅是一种化学元素，符号为 Si，原子序数为 14。它是一种四价类金属，比其下方直接相邻的类金属锗更活泼。关于硅的性质争论可以追溯到它被发现之时。

image.title: 超纯硅块，2 x 2 厘米
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/silicon.php

elements.summary_en: Silicon is a chemical element with symbol Si and atomic number 14. It is a tetravalent metalloid, more reactive than germanium, the metalloid directly below it in the table. Controversy about silicon's character dates to its discovery.

elements.name: 磷
elements.appearance: 无色、蜡白色、黄色、猩红色、红色、紫色、黑色
elements.summary: 磷是一种化学元素，符号为 P，原子序数为 15。作为一种元素，磷主要以两种形式存在——白磷和红磷——但由于其高度活泼，磷在地球上从未以游离元素的形式被发现。相反，含磷矿物几乎总是以其最高氧化态存在，即无机磷酸盐岩。

image.title: 紫磷
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/phosphorus.php

elements.summary_en: Phosphorus is a chemical element with symbol P and atomic number 15. As an element, phosphorus exists in two major forms—white phosphorus and red phosphorus—but due to its high reactivity, phosphorus is never found as a free element on Earth. Instead phosphorus-containing minerals are almost always present in their maximally oxidised state, as inorganic phosphate rocks.
elements.appearance_en: colourless, waxy white, yellow, scarlet, red, violet, black

elements.name: 硫
elements.summary: 硫（Sulfur 或 sulphur）是一种化学元素，符号为 S，原子序数为 16。它是一种丰度很高的多价非金属。在正常条件下，硫原子形成化学式为 S8 的环状八原子分子。

image.title: 来自俄罗斯的天然硫
image.attribution: James St. John, CC BY 2.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Sulfur or sulphur (see spelling differences) is a chemical element with symbol S and atomic number 16. It is an abundant, multivalent non-metal. Under normal conditions, sulfur atoms form cyclic octatomic molecules with chemical formula S8.

elements.name: 氯
elements.summary: 氯是一种化学元素，符号为 Cl，原子序数为 17。它的相对原子质量为 35.5。氯属于卤素组（17），是继氟之后第二轻的卤素。

image.title: 氯气样本
image.attribution: Benjah-bmm27, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Chlorine is a chemical element with symbol Cl and atomic number 17. It also has a relative atomic mass of 35.5. Chlorine is in the halogen group (17) and is the second lightest halogen following fluorine.

elements.name: 氩
elements.appearance: 无色气体，置于高电场中时呈现淡紫/紫色光泽
elements.summary: 氩是一种化学元素，符号为 Ar，原子序数为 18。它位于周期表的第 18 组，是一种稀有气体。氩是地球大气中第三常见的气体，占比 0.934% (9,340 ppmv)，其丰度是下一种最常见大气气体水蒸气（平均约 4000 ppmv，但波动很大）的两倍多，是下一种最常见非冷凝大气气体二氧化碳（400 ppmv）的 23 倍，是下一种最常见稀有气体氖（18 ppmv）的 500 多倍。

image.title: 装有发光的超纯氩气的小瓶。原始尺寸（厘米）：1 x 5
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/argon.php

elements.summary_en: Argon is a chemical element with symbol Ar and atomic number 18. It is in group 18 of the periodic table and is a noble gas. Argon is the third most common gas in the Earth's atmosphere, at 0.934% (9,340 ppmv), making it over twice as abundant as the next most common atmospheric gas, water vapor (which averages about 4000 ppmv, but varies greatly), and 23 times as abundant as the next most common non-condensing atmospheric gas, carbon dioxide (400 ppmv), and more than 500 times as abundant as the next most common noble gas, neon (18 ppmv).
elements.appearance_en: colorless gas exhibiting a lilac/violet glow when placed in a high voltage electric field

elements.name: 钾
elements.summary: 钾是一种化学元素，符号为 K（源自新拉丁语 kalium），原子序数为 19。它最初是从草木灰（potash）中提取出来的，这也是其名称的由来。在周期表中，钾是第 1 列（碱金属）的七个元素之一：它们的外层电子壳层都只有一个价电子，极易失去该电子以产生带正电荷的原子——阳离子，并与阴离子结合形成盐类。

image.title: 钾块
image.attribution: Dnn87, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Potassium is a chemical element with symbol K (derived from Neo-Latin, kalium) and atomic number 19. It was first isolated from potash, the ashes of plants, from which its name is derived. In the Periodic table, potassium is one of seven elements in column (group) 1 (alkali metals):they all have a single valence electron in their outer electron shell, which they readily give up to create an atom with a positive charge - a cation, and combine with anions to form salts.

elements.name: 钙
elements.summary: 钙是一种化学元素，符号为 Ca，原子序数为 20。钙是一种柔软的灰色碱土金属，是地壳中按质量计丰度第五高的元素。Ca2+ 离子也是海水中按摩尔浓度和质量计丰度第五高的溶解离子，仅次于钠、氯、镁和硫酸根。

image.title: 钙颗粒，粒径约 1 毫米
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/calcium.php

elements.summary_en: Calcium is a chemical element with symbol Ca and atomic number 20. Calcium is a soft gray alkaline earth metal, fifth-most-abundant element by mass in the Earth's crust. The ion Ca2+ is also the fifth-most-abundant dissolved ion in seawater by both molarity and mass, after sodium, chloride, magnesium, and sulfate.

elements.name: 钪
elements.discovered_by: 拉斯·弗雷德里克·尼尔森
elements.summary: 钪是一种化学元素，符号为 Sc，原子序数为 21。作为一种银白色的金属 d 区元素，它在历史上有时与钇和镧系元素一起被归类为稀土元素。它于 1879 年通过对来自斯堪的纳维亚的稀稀金矿和硅铍钇矿进行光谱分析而发现。

image.title: 钪晶体。约 1 克
image.attribution: JanDerChemiker, CC BY-SA 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Scandium is a chemical element with symbol Sc and atomic number 21. A silvery-white metallic d-block element, it has historically been sometimes classified as a rare earth element, together with yttrium and the lanthanoids. It was discovered in 1879 by spectral analysis of the minerals euxenite and gadolinite from Scandinavia.

elements.name: 钛
elements.named_by: 马丁·海因里希·克拉普罗特
elements.summary: 钛是一种化学元素，符号为 Ti，原子序数为 22。它是一种具有光泽的过渡金属，呈银色，密度低，强度高。它在海水、王水和氯气中具有极强的耐腐蚀性。

image.title: 通过范·阿克尔-德·波耳工艺制成的钛晶体。87 克，原始尺寸（厘米）：2.5 x 4
image.attribution: Hi-Res Images ofChemical Elements, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/titanium.php

elements.summary_en: Titanium is a chemical element with symbol Ti and atomic number 22. It is a lustrous transition metal with a silver color, low density and high strength. It is highly resistant to corrosion in sea water, aqua regia and chlorine.

elements.name: 钒
elements.discovered_by: 安德烈斯·曼努埃尔·德尔里奥
elements.named_by: 尼尔斯·加布里埃尔·塞夫斯特伦
elements.summary: 钒是一种化学元素，符号为 V，原子序数为 23。它是一种坚硬、银灰色、具有延展性和可塑性的过渡金属。该元素在自然界中仅以化学结合的形式存在，但一旦人工分离出来，氧化层的形成会使游离金属在一定程度上稳定，防止进一步氧化。

image.title: 带有氧化层的纯钒块
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/vanadium.php

elements.summary_en: Vanadium is a chemical element with symbol V and atomic number 23. It is a hard, silvery grey, ductile and malleable transition metal. The element is found only in chemically combined form in nature, but once isolated artificially, the formation of an oxide layer stabilizes the free metal somewhat against further oxidation.

elements.name: 铬
elements.summary: 铬是一种化学元素，符号为 Cr，原子序数为 24。它是第 6 组的第一个元素。它是一种钢灰色、有光泽、坚硬且易碎的金属，具有很高的抛光度，耐腐蚀，并且具有很高的熔点。

image.title: 铬金属块
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/chromium.php

elements.summary_en: Chromium is a chemical element with symbol Cr and atomic number 24. It is the first element in Group 6. It is a steely-gray, lustrous, hard and brittle metal which takes a high polish, resists tarnishing, and has a high melting point.

elements.name: 锰
elements.summary: 锰是一种化学元素，符号为 Mn，原子序数为 25。在自然界中不以游离元素形式存在；它通常与铁结合存在于许多矿物中。锰是一种具有重要工业金属合金用途的金属，特别是在不锈钢中。

image.title: 两块锰金属
image.attribution: W. Oelen, CC BY-SA 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Manganese is a chemical element with symbol Mn and atomic number 25. It is not found as a free element in nature; it is often found in combination with iron, and in many minerals. Manganese is a metal with important industrial metal alloy uses, particularly in stainless steels.

elements.name: 铁
elements.appearance: 有光泽的金属感，略带灰色
elements.summary: 铁是一种化学元素，符号为 Fe（源自拉丁语：ferrum），原子序数为 26。它是第一过渡系中的一种金属。按质量计算，它是地球上最常见的元素，构成了地球外核和内核的大部分。

image.title: 铁陨石碎片，含铁量约 92%。单个碎片的原始尺寸（厘米）：0.4 - 0.8
image.attribution: Chemical ELements A Virtual Museum, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0> source: https://images-of-elements.com/iron.php

elements.summary_en: Iron is a chemical element with symbol Fe (from Latin:ferrum) and atomic number 26. It is a metal in the first transition series. It is by mass the most common element on Earth, forming much of Earth's outer and inner core.
elements.appearance_en: lustrous metallic with a grayish tinge

elements.name: 钴
elements.appearance: 坚硬有光泽的灰色金属
elements.summary: 钴是一种化学元素，符号为 Co，原子序数为 27。与镍一样，地壳中的钴仅以化学结合形式存在，除了在天然陨铁合金中发现的少量沉积物。通过还原熔炼产生的游离元素是一种坚硬、有光泽、银灰色的金属。

image.title: 钴碎片，7 克和 4 克。原始尺寸（厘米）：2 x 2
image.attribution: Hi-Res Images ofChemical Elements, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/cobalt.php

elements.summary_en: Cobalt is a chemical element with symbol Co and atomic number 27. Like nickel, cobalt in the Earth's crust is found only in chemically combined form, save for small deposits found in alloys of natural meteoric iron. The free element, produced by reductive smelting, is a hard, lustrous, silver-gray metal.

elements.name: 镍
elements.appearance: 有光泽、金属感、银色且略带金色
elements.discovered_by: 阿克塞尔·弗雷德里克·克伦斯泰特
elements.summary: 镍是一种化学元素，符号为 Ni，原子序数为 28。它是一种银白色有光泽的金属，略带金色。镍属于过渡金属，坚硬且具有延展性。

image.title: 镍块
image.attribution: Materialscientist at English Wikipedia, CC BY-SA 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Nickel is a chemical element with symbol Ni and atomic number 28. It is a silvery-white lustrous metal with a slight golden tinge. Nickel belongs to the transition metals and is hard and ductile.
elements.appearance_en: lustrous, metallic, and silver with a gold tinge

elements.name: 铜
elements.summary: 铜是一种化学元素，符号为 Cu（源自拉丁语：cuprum），原子序数为 29。它是一种柔软、具有延展性和韧性的金属，具有极高的导热性和导电性。纯铜新暴露的表面呈红橙色。

image.title: 尺寸约 1 ½ 英寸（4 厘米）的天然铜宏观图
image.attribution: Native_Copper_Macro_Digon3.jpg: 'Jonathan Zander (Digon3)' derivative work: Materialscientist, CC BY-SA 2.5 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/2.5>, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Copper is a chemical element with symbol Cu (from Latin:cuprum) and atomic number 29. It is a soft, malleable and ductile metal with very high thermal and electrical conductivity. A freshly exposed surface of pure copper has a reddish-orange color.

elements.name: 锌
elements.summary: 锌，在商业上也称为白铅，是一种化学元素，符号为 Zn，原子序数为 30。它是周期表第 12 组的第一个元素。在某些方面，锌在化学性质上与镁相似：其离子大小相似，且其唯一的常见氧化态为 +2。

image.title: 30 克锌，正面和背面。原始尺寸（厘米）：3
image.attribution: Hi-Res Images ofChemical Elements, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/zinc.php

elements.summary_en: Zinc, in commerce also spelter, is a chemical element with symbol Zn and atomic number 30. It is the first element of group 12 of the periodic table. In some respects zinc is chemically similar to magnesium:its ion is of similar size and its only common oxidation state is +2.

elements.name: 镓
elements.discovered_by: 保罗·埃米尔·勒科克·德布瓦博德兰
elements.summary: 镓是一种化学元素，符号为 Ga，原子序数为 31。单质镓在自然界中不以游离形式存在，而是以三价镓化合物的形式微量存在于锌矿石和铝土矿中。镓是一种柔软的银色金属，单质镓在低温下是易碎的固体，在 29.76 °C (85.57 °F) 时熔化（略高于室温）。

image.title: 固体镓，新鲜状态以及在室温下放置一段时间（2个月）后
image.attribution: Hi-Res Images ofChemical Elements, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/gallium.php

elements.summary_en: Gallium is a chemical element with symbol Ga and atomic number 31. Elemental gallium does not occur in free form in nature, but as the gallium(III) compounds that are in trace amounts in zinc ores and in bauxite. Gallium is a soft, silvery metal, and elemental gallium is a brittle solid at low temperatures, and melts at 29.76 °C (85.57 °F) (slightly above room temperature).

elements.name: 锗
elements.summary: 锗是一种化学元素，符号为 Ge，原子序数为 32。它是一种有光泽、坚硬、灰白色的碳族类金属，在化学性质上与其同族邻居锡和硅相似。纯化后的锗是一种半导体，外观与单质硅最为相似。

image.title: 12 克多晶锗，2*3 厘米
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/germanium.php

elements.summary_en: Germanium is a chemical element with symbol Ge and atomic number 32. It is a lustrous, hard, grayish-white metalloid in the carbon group, chemically similar to its group neighbors tin and silicon. Purified germanium is a semiconductor, with an appearance most similar to elemental silicon.

elements.name: 砷
elements.summary: 砷是一种化学元素，符号为 As，原子序数为 33。砷存在于许多矿物中，通常与硫和金属结合，也以纯元素晶体的形式存在。砷是一种类金属。

image.title: 氩气保护下的超纯金属砷，1 - 2 克。每块的原始尺寸（厘米）：0.5 x 1
image.attribution: Hi-Res Images ofChemical Elements, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/arsenic.php

elements.summary_en: Arsenic is a chemical element with symbol As and atomic number 33. Arsenic occurs in many minerals, usually in conjunction with sulfur and metals, and also as a pure elemental crystal. Arsenic is a metalloid.

elements.name: 硒
elements.appearance: 黑色、红色和灰色（未图示）同素异形体
elements.discovered_by: 约恩斯·雅各布·贝采利乌斯
elements.summary: 硒是一种化学元素，符号为 Se，原子序数为 34。它是一种非金属，其性质介于周期表中同列相邻的氧族元素硫和碲之间。它在自然界中很少以单质状态存在，也很少作为纯矿物化合物存在。

image.title: 超纯黑色无定形硒，3 - 4 克。原始尺寸（厘米）：2
image.attribution: Hi-Res Images ofChemical Elements, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/selenium.php

elements.summary_en: Selenium is a chemical element with symbol Se and atomic number 34. It is a nonmetal with properties that are intermediate between those of its periodic table column-adjacent chalcogen elements sulfur and tellurium. It rarely occurs in its elemental state in nature, or as pure ore compounds.
elements.appearance_en: black, red, and gray (not pictured) allotropes

elements.name: 溴
elements.summary: 溴（源自古希腊语：βρῶμος, brómos，意为“恶臭”）是一种化学元素，符号为 Br，原子序数为 35。它是一种卤素。该元素由两位化学家卡尔·雅各布·勒维希和安托万·热罗姆·巴拉尔在 1825-1826 年独立分离出来。

image.title: 封装在丙烯酸中的 4 x 1 厘米大玻璃安瓿内的 99.5% 纯液溴
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/bromine.php

elements.summary_en: Bromine (from Ancient Greek:βρῶμος, brómos, meaning "stench") is a chemical element with symbol Br, and atomic number 35. It is a halogen. The element was isolated independently by two chemists, Carl Jacob Löwig and Antoine Jerome Balard, in 1825–1826.

elements.name: 氪
elements.appearance: 无色气体，在高电场中呈现白色光泽
elements.summary: 氪（源自希腊语：κρυπτός kryptos “隐藏者”）是一种化学元素，符号为 Kr，原子序数为 36。它是第 18 组（稀有气体）元素的成员。氪是一种无色、无嗅、无味的稀有气体，在大气中微量存在，通过分级蒸馏液态空气分离，常与其他稀有气体一起用于荧光灯中。

image.title: 装有发光的超纯氪气的小瓶。原始尺寸（厘米）：1 x 5
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/krypton.php

elements.summary_en: Krypton (from Greek:κρυπτός kryptos "the hidden one") is a chemical element with symbol Kr and atomic number 36. It is a member of group 18 (noble gases) elements. A colorless, odorless, tasteless noble gas, krypton occurs in trace amounts in the atmosphere, is isolated by fractionally distilling liquefied air, and is often used with other rare gases in fluorescent lamps.
elements.appearance_en: colorless gas, exhibiting a whitish glow in a high electric field

elements.name: 铷
elements.summary: 铷是一种化学元素，符号为 Rb，原子序数为 37。铷是碱金属组中一种柔软的银白色金属元素，原子质量为 85.4678。单质铷具有高度活性，其性质与其他碱金属相似，例如在空气中极易氧化。

image.title: 金属铷样本
image.attribution: Dnn87, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Rubidium is a chemical element with symbol Rb and atomic number 37. Rubidium is a soft, silvery-white metallic element of the alkali metal group, with an atomic mass of 85.4678. Elemental rubidium is highly reactive, with properties similar to those of other alkali metals, such as very rapid oxidation in air.

elements.name: 锶
elements.discovered_by: 威廉·克鲁克香克（化学家）
elements.summary: 锶是一种化学元素，符号为 Sr，原子序数为 38。作为一种碱土金属，锶是一种柔软的银白色或淡黄色金属元素，化学性质高度活泼。该金属暴露在空气中时会变黄。

image.title: 石蜡油下的锶块
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/strontium.php

elements.summary_en: Strontium is a chemical element with symbol Sr and atomic number 38. An alkaline earth metal, strontium is a soft silver-white or yellowish metallic element that is highly reactive chemically. The metal turns yellow when it is exposed to air.

elements.name: 钇
elements.summary: 钇是一种化学元素，符号为 Y，原子序数为 39。它是一种银色金属过渡金属，在化学性质上与镧系元素相似，常被归类为“稀土元素”。钇在稀土矿物中几乎总是与镧系元素结合存在，在自然界中从未以游离元素形式发现。

image.title: 6.21 克钇，纯度至少 99%
image.attribution: Jan Anskeit, CC BY-SA 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Yttrium is a chemical element with symbol Y and atomic number 39. It is a silvery-metallic transition metal chemically similar to the lanthanides and it has often been classified as a "rare earth element". Yttrium is almost always found combined with the lanthanides in rare earth minerals and is never found in nature as a free element.

elements.name: 锆
elements.discovered_by: 马丁·海因里希·克拉普罗特
elements.summary: 锆是一种化学元素，符号为 Zr，原子序数为 40。锆的名称取自矿物锆石（zircon），这是锆最重要的来源。zircon 一词来自波斯语 zargun زرگون，意为“金色的”。

image.title: 两块锆，每块 1 厘米
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/zirconium.php

elements.summary_en: Zirconium is a chemical element with symbol Zr and atomic number 40. The name of zirconium is taken from the name of the mineral zircon, the most important source of zirconium. The word zircon comes from the Persian word zargun زرگون, meaning "gold-colored".

elements.name: 铌
elements.appearance: 灰色金属感，氧化后带蓝色
elements.summary: 铌，以前称为 columbium，是一种化学元素，符号为 Nb（以前为 Cb），原子序数为 41。它是一种柔软、灰色、具有延展性的过渡金属，常发现于烧绿石矿（铌的主要商业来源）和铌铁矿中。其名称源自希腊神话：坦塔罗斯的女儿尼俄伯（Niobe），因为它与钽（tantalum）非常相似。

image.title: 铌条
image.attribution: Mauro Cateb, CC BY-SA 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Niobium, formerly columbium, is a chemical element with symbol Nb (formerly Cb) and atomic number 41. It is a soft, grey, ductile transition metal, which is often found in the pyrochlore mineral, the main commercial source for niobium, and columbite. The name comes from Greek mythology:Niobe, daughter of Tantalus since it is so similar to tantalum.

elements.name: 钼
elements.summary: 钼是一种化学元素，符号为 Mo，原子序数为 42。其名称源自新拉丁语 molybdaenum，源自古希腊语 Μόλυβδος molybdos，意为“铅”，因为其矿石曾被误认为是铅矿。钼矿物在历史上一直为人所知，但该元素（指将其作为一种新实体与其它金属盐区分开来）是由卡尔·威廉·舍勒在 1778 年发现的。

image.title: 99.9% 纯钼晶体，约 2 x 3 厘米，带有阳极氧化色
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/molybdenum.php

elements.summary_en: Molybdenum is a chemical element with symbol Mo and atomic number 42. The name is from Neo-Latin molybdaenum, from Ancient Greek Μόλυβδος molybdos, meaning lead, since its ores were confused with lead ores. Molybdenum minerals have been known throughout history, but the element was discovered (in the sense of differentiating it as a new entity from the mineral salts of other metals) in 1778 by Carl Wilhelm Scheele.

elements.name: 锝
elements.summary: 锝是一种化学元素，符号为 Tc，原子序数为 43。它是周期表中原子序数最低的没有稳定同位素的元素：它的每种形式都具有放射性。几乎所有的锝都是合成产生的，自然界中仅发现极微量。

image.title: 密封在充满氩气的玻璃安瓿内的锝样本。6x1 毫米的金箔，覆盖有 99Tc 粉末（电镀）。
image.attribution: GFDL, CC BY-SA 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Technetium (/tɛkˈniːʃiəm/) is a chemical element with symbol Tc and atomic number 43. It is the element with the lowest atomic number in the periodic table that has no stable isotopes:every form of it is radioactive. Nearly all technetium is produced synthetically, and only minute amounts are found in nature.

elements.name: 钌
elements.summary: 钌是一种化学元素，符号为 Ru，原子序数为 44。它是一种罕见的过渡金属，属于周期表中的铂族。与铂族的其他金属一样，钌对大多数其他化学物质呈惰性。

image.title: 钌晶体，0.6 克，尺寸 0.6 x 1.3 厘米
image.attribution: Hi-Res Images ofChemical Elements, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/ruthenium.php

elements.summary_en: Ruthenium is a chemical element with symbol Ru and atomic number 44. It is a rare transition metal belonging to the platinum group of the periodic table. Like the other metals of the platinum group, ruthenium is inert to most other chemicals.

elements.name: 铑
elements.summary: 铑是一种化学元素，符号为 Rh，原子序数为 45。它是一种稀有、银白色、坚硬且化学惰性的过渡金属。它是铂族的成员。

image.title: 纯铑珠，1 克。原始尺寸（厘米）：0.5
image.attribution: Hi-Res Images ofChemical Elements, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/rhodium.php

elements.summary_en: Rhodium is a chemical element with symbol Rh and atomic number 45. It is a rare, silvery-white, hard, and chemically inert transition metal. It is a member of the platinum group.

elements.name: 钯
elements.summary: 钯是一种化学元素，符号为 Pd，原子序数为 46。它是一种稀有且有光泽的银白色金属，由威廉·海德·沃拉斯顿于 1803 年发现。他以小行星智神星（Pallas）命名，而该小行星本身是以希腊女神雅典娜的绰号命名的，雅典娜在杀死帕拉斯（Pallas）后获得了这个绰号。

image.title: 钯晶体，约 1 克。原始尺寸（厘米）：0.5 x 1
image.attribution: Hi-Res Images ofChemical Elements, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/palladium.php

elements.summary_en: Palladium is a chemical element with symbol Pd and atomic number 46. It is a rare and lustrous silvery-white metal discovered in 1803 by William Hyde Wollaston. He named it after the asteroid Pallas, which was itself named after the epithet of the Greek goddess Athena, acquired by her when she slew Pallas.

elements.name: 银
elements.discovered_by: 未知，公元前 5000 年以前
elements.summary: 银是一种化学元素，符号为 Ag（源自希腊语：άργυρος árguros，拉丁语：argentum，两者都源自印欧语系词根 *h₂erǵ-，意为“灰色”或“闪亮的”），原子序数为 47。它是一种柔软、白色、有光泽的过渡金属，在所有金属中具有最高的电导率、热导率和反射率。该金属在自然界中以纯净游离形式（天然银）、与金和其他金属形成的合金以及在角银矿和辉银矿等矿物中存在。

image.title: 天然银块，1 厘米长
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: http://images-of-elements.com/silver.php

elements.summary_en: Silver is a chemical element with symbol Ag (Greek:άργυρος árguros, Latin:argentum, both from the Indo-European root *h₂erǵ- for "grey" or "shining") and atomic number 47. A soft, white, lustrous transition metal, it possesses the highest electrical conductivity, thermal conductivity and reflectivity of any metal. The metal occurs naturally in its pure, free form (native silver), as an alloy with gold and other metals, and in minerals such as argentite and chlorargyrite.

elements.name: 镉
elements.appearance: 银色带蓝灰色的金属感
elements.discovered_by: 卡尔·萨缪尔·莱贝雷希特·赫尔曼
elements.summary: 镉是一种化学元素，符号为 Cd，原子序数为 48。这种柔软、蓝白色的金属在化学性质上与第 12 组的其他两种稳定金属锌和汞相似。与锌一样，它在大多数化合物中倾向于 +2 氧化态；与汞一样，与过渡金属相比，它的熔点较低。

image.title: 48 Cd 镉
image.attribution: Chemical Elements A Virtual Museum under a Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported License, source: https://images-of-elements.com/cadmium.php

elements.summary_en: Cadmium is a chemical element with symbol Cd and atomic number 48. This soft, bluish-white metal is chemically similar to the two other stable metals in group 12, zinc and mercury. Like zinc, it prefers oxidation state +2 in most of its compounds and like mercury it shows a low melting point compared to transition metals.

elements.name: 铟
elements.summary: 铟是一种化学元素，符号为 In，原子序数为 49。它是一种在地壳中稀有的后过渡金属元素。该金属非常柔软、具有延展性且易熔，熔点高于钠，但低于锂或锡。

image.title: 1.5 x 1.5 厘米液态铟
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: http://images-of-elements.com/indium.php

elements.summary_en: Indium is a chemical element with symbol In and atomic number 49. It is a post-transition metallic element that is rare in Earth's crust. The metal is very soft, malleable and easily fusible, with a melting point higher than sodium, but lower than lithium or tin.

elements.name: 锡
elements.discovered_by: 未知，公元前 3500 年以前
elements.summary: 锡是一种化学元素，符号为 Sn（源自拉丁语：stannum），原子序数为 50。它是周期表第 14 组的主族金属。锡与同为第 14 组的邻近元素锗和铅在化学性质上具有相似性，并具有两种可能的氧化态：+2 和略微更稳定的 +4。

image.title: 锡滴
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: http://images-of-elements.com/tin.php

elements.summary_en: Tin is a chemical element with the symbol Sn (for Latin:stannum) and atomic number 50. It is a main group metal in group 14 of the periodic table. Tin shows a chemical similarity to both neighboring group-14 elements, germanium and lead, and has two possible oxidation states, +2 and the slightly more stable +4.

elements.name: 锑
elements.discovered_by: 未知，公元前 3000 年以前
elements.summary: 锑是一种化学元素，符号为 Sb（源自拉丁语：stibium），原子序数为 51。它是一种有光泽的灰色类金属，在自然界中主要以硫化物矿物辉锑矿（Sb2S3）的形式存在。锑化合物自古以来就为人所知，并被用于化妆品；金属锑在发现时也为人所知，但曾被错误地鉴定为铅。

image.title: 锑晶体，2 克，1 厘米
image.attribution: Unknown authorUnknown author, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/antimony.php

elements.summary_en: Antimony is a chemical element with symbol Sb (from Latin:stibium) and atomic number 51. A lustrous gray metalloid, it is found in nature mainly as the sulfide mineral stibnite (Sb2S3). Antimony compounds have been known since ancient times and were used for cosmetics; metallic antimony was also known, but it was erroneously identified as lead upon its discovery.

elements.name: 碲
elements.discovered_by: 弗朗茨-约瑟夫·米勒·冯·赖兴施泰因
elements.summary: 碲是一种化学元素，符号为 Te，原子序数为 52。它是一种脆性、轻微有毒、罕见的银白色类金属。碲在化学性质上与硒和硫相关。

image.title: 金属碲，直径 3.5 厘米
image.attribution: Unknown authorUnknown author, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/tellurium.php

elements.summary_en: Tellurium is a chemical element with symbol Te and atomic number 52. It is a brittle, mildly toxic, rare, silver-white metalloid. Tellurium is chemically related to selenium and sulfur.

elements.name: 碘
elements.appearance: 有光泽的金属灰色，气体时为紫色
elements.summary: 碘是一种化学元素，符号为 I，原子序数为 53。其名称源自希腊语 ἰοειδής ioeidēs，意为紫色，这是由于碘蒸气的颜色。碘及其化合物主要用于营养领域，工业上则用于生产乙酸和某些聚合物。

image.title: 碘样本
image.attribution: Benjah-bmm27, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Iodine is a chemical element with symbol I and atomic number 53. The name is from Greek ἰοειδής ioeidēs, meaning violet or purple, due to the color of iodine vapor. Iodine and its compounds are primarily used in nutrition, and industrially in the production of acetic acid and certain polymers.
elements.appearance_en: lustrous metallic gray, violet as a gas

elements.name: 氙
elements.appearance: 无色气体，置于高压电场中时呈现蓝色光泽
elements.summary: 氙是一种化学元素，符号为 Xe，原子序数为 54。它是一种无色、致密、无嗅的稀有气体，在地球大气中微量存在。虽然通常不活泼，但氙可以发生一些化学反应，例如形成六氟合铂酸氙，这是合成的第一种稀有气体化合物。

image.title: 装有发光的超纯氙气的小瓶。原始尺寸（厘米）：1 x 5
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/xenon.php

elements.summary_en: Xenon is a chemical element with symbol Xe and atomic number 54. It is a colorless, dense, odorless noble gas, that occurs in the Earth's atmosphere in trace amounts. Although generally unreactive, xenon can undergo a few chemical reactions such as the formation of xenon hexafluoroplatinate, the first noble gas compound to be synthesized.
elements.appearance_en: colorless gas, exhibiting a blue glow when placed in a high voltage electric field

elements.name: 铯
elements.summary: 铯是一种化学元素，符号为 Cs，原子序数为 55。它是一种柔软的银金色碱金属，熔点为 28 °C (82 °F)，这使其成为仅有的五种在室温或接近室温下呈液态的金属元素之一。铯是一种碱金属，其物理和化学性质与铷和钾相似。

image.title: 铯金属
image.attribution: Dnn87 Contact email: Dnn87yahoo.dk, CC BY-SA 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Caesium or cesium is a chemical element with symbol Cs and atomic number 55. It is a soft, silvery-gold alkali metal with a melting point of 28 °C (82 °F), which makes it one of only five elemental metals that are liquid at or near room temperature. Caesium is an alkali metal and has physical and chemical properties similar to those of rubidium and potassium.

elements.name: 钡
elements.summary: 钡是一种化学元素，符号为 Ba，原子序数为 56。它是第 2 组中的第五个元素，是一种柔软的银色金属碱土金属。由于其高度的化学活性，钡在自然界中从未以游离元素形式发现。

image.title: 氩气保护下带有灰色氧化层的 1.5 克钡。原始尺寸（厘米）：0.7 x 1
image.attribution: Hi-Res Images ofChemical Elements, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/barium.php

elements.summary_en: Barium is a chemical element with symbol Ba and atomic number 56. It is the fifth element in Group 2, a soft silvery metallic alkaline earth metal. Because of its high chemical reactivity barium is never found in nature as a free element.

elements.name: 镧
elements.discovered_by: 卡尔·古斯塔夫·莫桑德尔
elements.summary: 镧是一种柔软、具有延展性的银白色金属化学元素，符号为 La，原子序数为 57。它暴露在空气中会迅速失去光泽，质地柔软，可以用刀切割。它是镧系元素的命名来源，镧系元素是周期表中镧和镥之间的一组 15 个相似元素：它有时也被认为是第 6 周期过渡金属的第一个元素。

image.title: 1 厘米大的纯镧块
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/lanthanum.php

elements.summary_en: Lanthanum is a soft, ductile, silvery-white metallic chemical element with symbol La and atomic number 57. It tarnishes rapidly when exposed to air and is soft enough to be cut with a knife. It gave its name to the lanthanide series, a group of 15 similar elements between lanthanum and lutetium in the periodic table:it is also sometimes considered the first element of the 6th-period transition metals.

elements.name: 铈
elements.discovered_by: 马丁·海因里希·克拉普罗特
elements.summary: 铈是一种化学元素，符号为 Ce，原子序数为 58。它是一种柔软、银色、具有延展性的金属，在空气中容易氧化。铈是以矮行星谷神星（Ceres，本身以罗马农业女神命名）命名的。

image.title: 氩气保护下的超纯铈，1.5 克。原始尺寸（厘米）：1 x 1
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 1.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/1.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/cerium.php

elements.summary_en: Cerium is a chemical element with symbol Ce and atomic number 58. It is a soft, silvery, ductile metal which easily oxidizes in air. Cerium was named after the dwarf planet Ceres (itself named after the Roman goddess of agriculture).

elements.name: 镨
elements.discovered_by: 卡尔·奥尔·冯·韦尔斯巴赫
elements.summary: 镨是一种化学元素，符号为 Pr，原子序数为 59。镨是镧系元素中一种柔软、银色、具有延展性和韧性的金属。它因其磁学、电学、化学和光学性质而受到重视。

image.title: 氩气保护下的 1.5 克镨，0.5 厘米大的碎片
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/praseodymium.php

elements.summary_en: Praseodymium is a chemical element with symbol Pr and atomic number 59. Praseodymium is a soft, silvery, malleable and ductile metal in the lanthanide group. It is valued for its magnetic, electrical, chemical, and optical properties.

elements.name: 钕
elements.discovered_by: 卡尔·奥尔·冯·韦尔斯巴赫
elements.summary: 钕是一种化学元素，符号为 Nd，原子序数为 60。它是一种在空气中会失去光泽的柔软银色金属。钕于 1885 年由奥地利化学家卡尔·奥尔·冯·韦尔斯巴赫发现。

image.title: 氩气保护下的超纯钕，5 克。大块的原始长度（厘米）：1
image.attribution: Hi-Res Images ofChemical Elements, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/neodymium.php

elements.summary_en: Neodymium is a chemical element with symbol Nd and atomic number 60. It is a soft silvery metal that tarnishes in air. Neodymium was discovered in 1885 by the Austrian chemist Carl Auer von Welsbach.

elements.name: 钷
elements.summary: 钷，最初为 prometheum，是一种化学元素，符号为 Pm，原子序数为 61。它的所有同位素都具有放射性；它是周期表中仅有的两个此类元素之一，其后紧随具有稳定形式的元素，这一特点与锝共有。在化学上，钷是一种镧系元素，与其他元素结合时形成盐类。

image.title: 金属钷可能外观的蒙太奇照片（它放射性太强，无法获得真实图像）
image.attribution: Unknown authorUnknown author, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/promethium.php

elements.summary_en: Promethium, originally prometheum, is a chemical element with the symbol Pm and atomic number 61. All of its isotopes are radioactive; it is one of only two such elements that are followed in the periodic table by elements with stable forms, a distinction shared with technetium. Chemically, promethium is a lanthanide, which forms salts when combined with other elements.

elements.name: 钐
elements.discovered_by: 保罗·埃米尔·勒科克·德布瓦博德兰
elements.summary: 钐是一种化学元素，符号为 Sm，原子序数为 62。它是一种中等硬度的银色金属，在空气中容易氧化。作为镧系元素的典型成员，钐通常呈现 +3 氧化态。

image.title: 超纯升华钐，2 克。原始尺寸（厘米）：0.8 x 1.5
image.attribution: Unknown authorUnknown author, CC BY 1.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/1.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/samarium.php

elements.summary_en: Samarium is a chemical element with symbol Sm and atomic number 62. It is a moderately hard silvery metal that readily oxidizes in air. Being a typical member of the lanthanide series, samarium usually assumes the oxidation state +3.

elements.name: 铕
elements.summary: 铕是一种化学元素，符号为 Eu，原子序数为 63。它于 1901 年被分离出来，并以欧洲大陆命名。它是一种中等硬度的银色金属，在空气和水中容易氧化。

image.title: 轻微氧化的铕，因此略显黄色。1.5 克，大块尺寸 0.6 x 1.6 厘米。
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/europium.php

elements.summary_en: Europium is a chemical element with symbol Eu and atomic number 63. It was isolated in 1901 and is named after the continent of Europe. It is a moderately hard, silvery metal which readily oxidizes in air and water.

elements.name: 钆
elements.discovered_by: 让·夏尔·加利萨·德马里尼亚克
elements.summary: 钆是一种化学元素，符号为 Gd，原子序数为 64。它是一种银白色、具有延展性和韧性的稀土金属。它在自然界中仅以结合（盐）形式存在。

image.title: 纯 (99.95%) 无定形钆，约 12 克，2 × 1.5 × 0.5 厘米，封装在丙烯酸玻璃中
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/gadolinium.php

elements.summary_en: Gadolinium is a chemical element with symbol Gd and atomic number 64. It is a silvery-white, malleable and ductile rare-earth metal. It is found in nature only in combined (salt) form.

elements.name: 铽
elements.discovered_by: 卡尔·古斯塔夫·莫桑德尔
elements.summary: 铽是一种化学元素，符号为 Tb，原子序数为 65。它是一种银白色的稀土金属，具有延展性、韧性，且质地柔软，可以用刀切割。铽在自然界中从未以游离元素形式发现，但它包含在许多矿物中，包括铈石、硅铍钇矿、独居石、磷钇矿和稀稀金矿。

image.title: 纯铽，3 克。原始尺寸：1 厘米
image.attribution: Unknown authorUnknown author, CC BY 1.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/1.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/terbium.php

elements.summary_en: Terbium is a chemical element with symbol Tb and atomic number 65. It is a silvery-white rare earth metal that is malleable, ductile and soft enough to be cut with a knife. Terbium is never found in nature as a free element, but it is contained in many minerals, including cerite, gadolinite, monazite, xenotime and euxenite.

elements.name: 镝
elements.discovered_by: 保罗·埃米尔·勒科克·德布瓦博德兰
elements.summary: 镝是一种化学元素，符号为 Dy，原子序数为 66。它是一种具有金属银色光泽的稀土元素。镝在自然界中从未以游离元素形式发现，尽管它存在于各种矿物中，如磷钇矿。

image.title: 纯镝树枝状晶体
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/dysprosium.php

elements.summary_en: Dysprosium is a chemical element with the symbol Dy and atomic number 66. It is a rare earth element with a metallic silver luster. Dysprosium is never found in nature as a free element, though it is found in various minerals, such as xenotime.

elements.name: 钬
elements.summary: 钬是一种化学元素，符号为 Ho，原子序数为 67。作为镧系元素的一部分，钬是一种稀土元素。钬由瑞典化学家佩尔·提奥多·克莱夫发现。

image.title: 超纯钬，17 克。原始尺寸（厘米）：1.5 x 2.5
image.attribution: Unknown authorUnknown author, CC BY 1.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/1.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/holmium.php

elements.summary_en: Holmium is a chemical element with symbol Ho and atomic number 67. Part of the lanthanide series, holmium is a rare earth element. Holmium was discovered by Swedish chemist Per Theodor Cleve.

elements.name: 铒
elements.discovered_by: 卡尔·古斯塔夫·莫桑德尔
elements.summary: 铒是镧系元素中的一种化学元素，符号为 Er，原子序数为 68。人工分离时为银白色固体金属，天然铒在地球上总是以与其他元素结合的化学形式存在。因此，它是一种稀土元素，与瑞典伊特比的硅铍钇矿中的其他几种稀有元素相关联，在那里还发现了钇、镱和铽。

image.title: 9.5 克纯铒，2 x 2 厘米
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/erbium.php

elements.summary_en: Erbium is a chemical element in the lanthanide series, with symbol Er and atomic number 68. A silvery-white solid metal when artificially isolated, natural erbium is always found in chemical combination with other elements on Earth. As such, it is a rare earth element which is associated with several other rare elements in the mineral gadolinite from Ytterby in Sweden, where yttrium, ytterbium, and terbium were discovered.

elements.name: 铥
elements.summary: 铥是一种化学元素，符号为 Tm，原子序数为 69。它是镧系元素中的第十三个，也是倒数第三个元素。与其他镧系元素一样，最常见的氧化态是 +3，见于其氧化物、卤化物和其他化合物中。

image.title: 超纯 (99.997%) 结晶铥，22.3 克，尺寸 3 × 3 × 2 厘米
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/thulium.php

elements.summary_en: Thulium is a chemical element with symbol Tm and atomic number 69. It is the thirteenth and antepenultimate (third-last) element in the lanthanide series. Like the other lanthanides, the most common oxidation state is +3, seen in its oxide, halides and other compounds.

elements.name: 镱
elements.discovered_by: 让·夏尔·加利萨·德马里尼亚克
elements.summary: 镱是一种化学元素，符号为 Yb，原子序数为 70。它是镧系元素中的第十四个也是倒数第二个元素，这是其 +2 氧化态相对稳定的基础。然而，与其他镧系元素一样，其最常见的氧化态是 +3，见于其氧化物、卤化物和其他化合物中。

image.title: 镱，0.5 x 1 厘米
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 1.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/1.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/ytterbium.php

elements.summary_en: Ytterbium is a chemical element with symbol Yb and atomic number 70. It is the fourteenth and penultimate element in the lanthanide series, which is the basis of the relative stability of its +2 oxidation state. However, like the other lanthanides, its most common oxidation state is +3, seen in its oxide, halides and other compounds.

elements.name: 镥
elements.summary: 镥是一种化学元素，符号为 Lu，原子序数为 71。它是一种银白色金属，在干燥空气中耐腐蚀，但在潮湿空气中不耐。它被认为是第 6 周期过渡金属的第一个元素，也是镧系元素中的最后一个元素，传统上被列为稀土元素。

image.title: 1 厘米大的纯镥块
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/lutetium.php

elements.summary_en: Lutetium is a chemical element with symbol Lu and atomic number 71. It is a silvery white metal, which resists corrosion in dry, but not in moist air. It is considered the first element of the 6th-period transition metals and the last element in the lanthanide series, and is traditionally counted among the rare earths.

elements.name: 铪
elements.summary: 铪是一种化学元素，符号为 Hf，原子序数为 72。铪是一种有光泽、银灰色、四价过渡金属，在化学性质上与锆相似，并发现于锆矿物中。德米特里·门捷列夫于 1869 年预言了它的存在，但直到 1923 年才被鉴定出来，使其成为倒数第二个被发现的稳定元素（铼在两年后被鉴定出来）。

image.title: 电解铪，22 克。原始尺寸（厘米）：1 x 2 x 3
image.attribution: Hi-Res Images ofChemical Elements, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/hafnium.php

elements.summary_en: Hafnium is a chemical element with symbol Hf and atomic number 72. A lustrous, silvery gray, tetravalent transition metal, hafnium chemically resembles zirconium and is found in zirconium minerals. Its existence was predicted by Dmitri Mendeleev in 1869, though it was not identified until 1923, making it the penultimate stable element to be discovered (rhenium was identified two years later).

elements.name: 钽
elements.discovered_by: 安德斯·古斯塔夫·埃克贝里
elements.summary: 钽是一种化学元素，符号为 Ta，原子序数为 73。以前称为 tantalium，其名称源自希腊神话中的反英雄坦塔罗斯（Tantalus）。钽是一种罕见、坚硬、蓝灰色、有光泽的过渡金属，具有极高的耐腐蚀性。

image.title: 钽片，尺寸 1 厘米
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/tantalum.php

elements.summary_en: Tantalum is a chemical element with symbol Ta and atomic number 73. Previously known as tantalium, its name comes from Tantalus, an antihero from Greek mythology. Tantalum is a rare, hard, blue-gray, lustrous transition metal that is highly corrosion-resistant.

elements.name: 钨
elements.summary: 钨，也称为 wolfram，是一种化学元素，符号为 W，原子序数为 74。tungsten 一词源自瑞典语 tung sten，直接翻译为“重石”。它在瑞典语中的名称是 volfram，然而，为了将其与白钨矿区分开来，白钨矿在瑞典语中也被称为 tungsten。

image.title: 表面氧化的钨棒，80 克。原始尺寸（厘米）：1.3 x 3
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 1.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/1.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/tungsten.php

elements.summary_en: Tungsten, also known as wolfram, is a chemical element with symbol W and atomic number 74. The word tungsten comes from the Swedish language tung sten, which directly translates to heavy stone. Its name in Swedish is volfram, however, in order to distinguish it from scheelite, which in Swedish is alternatively named tungsten.

elements.name: 铼
elements.summary: 铼是一种化学元素，符号为 Re，原子序数为 75。它是一种银白色、沉重的第三行过渡金属，位于周期表的第 7 组。据估计平均浓度为十亿分之一 (ppb)，铼是地壳中最稀有的元素之一。

image.title: 纯铼珠，电弧熔化，21 克。原始尺寸（厘米）：1.5 x 1.7。测得辐射剂量 <0.05 μS/h。
image.attribution: Hi-Res Images ofChemical Elements, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/rhenium.php

elements.summary_en: Rhenium is a chemical element with symbol Re and atomic number 75. It is a silvery-white, heavy, third-row transition metal in group 7 of the periodic table. With an estimated average concentration of 1 part per billion (ppb), rhenium is one of the rarest elements in the Earth's crust.

elements.name: 锇
elements.summary: 锇（源自希腊语 osme (ὀσμή)，意为“气味”）是一种化学元素，符号为 Os，原子序数为 76。它是一种坚硬、易碎、蓝白色的过渡金属，属于铂族，在合金中作为微量元素存在，主要存在于铂矿石中。锇是密度最大的天然元素，密度为 22.59 g/cm3。

image.title: 纯锇珠
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/osmium.php

elements.summary_en: Osmium (from Greek osme (ὀσμή) meaning "smell") is a chemical element with symbol Os and atomic number 76. It is a hard, brittle, bluish-white transition metal in the platinum group that is found as a trace element in alloys, mostly in platinum ores. Osmium is the densest naturally occurring element, with a density of 22.59 g/cm3.

elements.name: 铱
elements.summary: 铱是一种化学元素，符号为 Ir，原子序数为 77。铱是一种非常坚硬、易碎、银白色的铂族过渡金属，根据测量密度，铱通常被认为是密度第二大的元素（仅次于锇），尽管涉及元素空间点阵的计算表明铱的密度更大。它也是最耐腐蚀的金属，即使在高达 2000 °C 的温度下也是如此。虽然只有某些熔盐和卤素对固体铱有腐蚀性，但细分的铱粉末活性要高得多，并且可能具有可燃性。

image.title: 纯铱块，1 克。原始尺寸：每个约 0.1 - 0.3 厘米
image.attribution: Unknown authorUnknown author, CC BY 1.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/1.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/iridium.php

elements.summary_en: Iridium is a chemical element with symbol Ir and atomic number 77. A very hard, brittle, silvery-white transition metal of the platinum group, iridium is generally credited with being the second densest element (after osmium) based on measured density, although calculations involving the space lattices of the elements show that iridium is denser. It is also the most corrosion-resistant metal, even at temperatures as high as 2000 °C. Although only certain molten salts and halogens are corrosive to solid iridium, finely divided iridium dust is much more reactive and can be flammable.

elements.name: 铂
elements.summary: 铂是一种化学元素，符号为 Pt，原子序数为 78。它是一种致密、具有延展性、韧性、高度不活泼、珍贵的灰白色过渡金属。其名称源自西班牙语术语 platina，字面翻译为“小银”。

image.title: 通过气相传输生长的纯铂晶体
image.attribution: Periodictableru, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Platinum is a chemical element with symbol Pt and atomic number 78. It is a dense, malleable, ductile, highly unreactive, precious, gray-white transition metal. Its name is derived from the Spanish term platina, which is literally translated into "little silver".

elements.name: 金
elements.summary: 金是一种化学元素，符号为 Au（源自拉丁语：aurum），原子序数为 79。在最纯净的形式下，它是一种明亮、略带红黄色、致密、柔软、具有延展性和韧性的金属。在化学上，金是一种过渡金属，属于第 11 组元素。

image.title: 超纯金箔
image.attribution: Hi-Res Images ofChemical Elements, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/gold.php

elements.summary_en: Gold is a chemical element with symbol Au (from Latin:aurum) and atomic number 79. In its purest form, it is a bright, slightly reddish yellow, dense, soft, malleable and ductile metal. Chemically, gold is a transition metal and a group 11 element.

elements.name: 汞
elements.discovered_by: 未知，公元前 2000 年以前
elements.summary: 汞是一种化学元素，符号为 Hg，原子序数为 80。它通常被称为水银，以前名为 hydrargyrum (/haɪˈdrɑːrdʒərəm/)。作为一种沉重的银色 d 区元素，汞是标准温度和压力条件下唯一呈液态的金属元素；在这种条件下唯一另一种呈液态的元素是溴，尽管铯、镓和铷等金属在略高于室温时也会熔化。

image.title: 6 克纯汞。内盘直径：2 厘米
image.attribution: Hi-Res Images of Chemical Elements, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/mercury.php

elements.summary_en: Mercury is a chemical element with symbol Hg and atomic number 80. It is commonly known as quicksilver and was formerly named hydrargyrum (/haɪˈdrɑːrdʒərəm/). A heavy, silvery d-block element, mercury is the only metallic element that is liquid at standard conditions for temperature and pressure; the only other element that is liquid under these conditions is bromine, though metals such as caesium, gallium, and rubidium melt just above room temperature.

elements.name: 铊
elements.summary: 铊是一种化学元素，符号为 Tl，原子序数为 81。这种柔软的灰色后过渡金属在自然界中不以游离形式存在。分离出来时，它类似于锡，但暴露在空气中会变色。

image.title: 氩气保护下的 8 克纯铊。原始尺寸（厘米）：0.7 x 1.5
image.attribution: Hi-Res Images ofChemical Elements, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/thallium.php

elements.summary_en: Thallium is a chemical element with symbol Tl and atomic number 81. This soft gray post-transition metal is not found free in nature. When isolated, it resembles tin, but discolors when exposed to air.

elements.name: 铅
elements.summary: 铅是一种碳族化学元素，符号为 Pb（源自拉丁语：plumbum），原子序数为 82。铅是一种柔软、具有延展性且沉重的后过渡金属。金属铅在刚切开时呈蓝白色，但暴露在空气中很快就会失去光泽，变成暗灰色。

image.title: 两面展示的超纯铅珠。原始尺寸（厘米）：1.5 x 2
image.attribution: Chemical Elements, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/lead.php

elements.summary_en: Lead (/lɛd/) is a chemical element in the carbon group with symbol Pb (from Latin:plumbum) and atomic number 82. Lead is a soft, malleable and heavy post-transition metal. Metallic lead has a bluish-white color after being freshly cut, but it soon tarnishes to a dull grayish color when exposed to air.

elements.name: 铋
elements.discovered_by: 克劳德·弗朗索瓦·若弗鲁瓦
elements.summary: 铋是一种化学元素，符号为 Bi，原子序数为 83。铋是一种五价后过渡金属，在化学性质上与砷和锑相似。单质铋可能天然存在，尽管其硫化物和氧化物构成了重要的商业矿石。

image.title: 铋晶体
image.attribution: Jurii, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://images-of-elements.com/bismuth.php

elements.summary_en: Bismuth is a chemical element with symbol Bi and atomic number 83. Bismuth, a pentavalent post-transition metal, chemically resembles arsenic and antimony. Elemental bismuth may occur naturally, although its sulfide and oxide form important commercial ores.

elements.name: 钋
elements.summary: 钋是一种化学元素，符号为 Po，原子序数为 84，由玛丽·居里和皮埃尔·居里于 1898 年发现。钋是一种罕见且具有高度放射性的元素，没有稳定同位素，在化学性质上与铋和碲相似，存在于铀矿石中。钋的应用很少。

image.title: 这只是插图，不是钋本身。一种银色放射性金属，产生的热量足以使其液化并使周围空气电离
image.attribution: Chemical ELements A Virtual Museum, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0> source: https://images-of-elements.com/polonium.php

elements.summary_en: Polonium is a chemical element with symbol Po and atomic number 84, discovered in 1898 by Marie Curie and Pierre Curie. A rare and highly radioactive element with no stable isotopes, polonium is chemically similar to bismuth and tellurium, and it occurs in uranium ores. Applications of polonium are few.

elements.name: 砹
elements.summary: 砹是一种非常稀有的放射性化学元素，化学符号为 At，原子序数为 85。它在地球上作为各种较重元素的衰变产物出现。其所有同位素的寿命都很短；最稳定的是砹-210，半衰期为 8.1 小时。

image.title: 这只是插图，不是砹本身。晶体类似于碘，但颜色更深；由于极强的放射性，它们会发出蓝光并蒸发成深紫色气体。
image.attribution: Chemical ELements A Virtual Museum, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0> source: https://images-of-elements.com/astatine.php

elements.summary_en: Astatine is a very rare radioactive chemical element with the chemical symbol At and atomic number 85. It occurs on Earth as the decay product of various heavier elements. All its isotopes are short-lived; the most stable is astatine-210, with a half-life of 8.1 hours.

elements.name: 氡
elements.appearance: 无色气体，在放电管中偶尔发出绿色或红色的光
elements.discovered_by: 弗里德里希·恩斯特·多恩
elements.summary: 氡是一种化学元素，符号为 Rn，原子序数为 86。它是一种放射性、无色、无味、无嗅的稀有气体，自然界中作为镭的衰变产物出现。其最稳定的同位素 222Rn 的半衰期为 3.8 天。

image.title: 这只是插图，不是氡本身。据说氡在放电管中会发出红光，但由于其强放射性，实际上几乎从不用于此目的。
image.attribution: Chemical ELements A Virtual Museum, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0> source: https://images-of-elements.com/radon.php

elements.summary_en: Radon is a chemical element with symbol Rn and atomic number 86. It is a radioactive, colorless, odorless, tasteless noble gas, occurring naturally as a decay product of radium. Its most stable isotope, 222Rn, has a half-life of 3.8 days.
elements.appearance_en: colorless gas, occasionally glows green or red in discharge tubes

elements.name: 钫
elements.summary: 钫是一种化学元素，符号为 Fr，原子序数为 87。它曾被称为类铯或锕 K。它是电负性第二低的元素，仅次于铯。钫是一种高度放射性的金属，会衰变为砹、镭和氡。

image.title: 这只是插图，不是钫本身。
image.attribution: Chemical ELements A Virtual Museum, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0> source: https://images-of-elements.com/francium.jpg

elements.summary_en: Francium is a chemical element with symbol Fr and atomic number 87. It used to be known as eka-caesium and actinium K. It is the second-least electronegative element, behind only caesium. Francium is a highly radioactive metal that decays into astatine, radium, and radon.

elements.name: 镭
elements.summary: 镭是一种化学元素，符号为 Ra，原子序数为 88。它是周期表第 2 族的第六个元素，属于碱土金属。纯镭几乎是无色的，但暴露在空气中时极易与氮气（而非氧气）结合，形成黑色的氮化镭 (Ra3N2) 表面层。

image.title: 镭电镀在极小的一块铜箔上，并覆盖聚氨酯以防止与空气反应
image.attribution: grenadier, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Radium is a chemical element with symbol Ra and atomic number 88. It is the sixth element in group 2 of the periodic table, also known as the alkaline earth metals. Pure radium is almost colorless, but it readily combines with nitrogen (rather than oxygen) on exposure to air, forming a black surface layer of radium nitride (Ra3N2).

elements.name: 锕
elements.discovered_by: 弗里德里希·奥斯卡·吉塞尔
elements.summary: 锕是一种放射性化学元素，符号为 Ac，原子序数为 89，于 1899 年发现。它是第一个被分离出来的非原生放射性元素。虽然钋、镭和氡在锕之前就被观察到了，但直到 1902 年它们才被分离出来。

image.title: 保存在橡树岭国家实验室 V 型瓶中的锕-225 医学放射性同位素。蓝色光芒来自 α 粒子对周围空气的电离。
image.attribution: Oak Ridge National Laboratory, CC BY 2.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0>, via Wikimedia Commons, source: https://www.flickr.com/photos/oakridgelab/31481701837/

elements.summary_en: Actinium is a radioactive chemical element with symbol Ac (not to be confused with the abbreviation for an acetyl group) and atomic number 89, which was discovered in 1899. It was the first non-primordial radioactive element to be isolated. Polonium, radium and radon were observed before actinium, but they were not isolated until 1902.

elements.name: 钍
elements.discovered_by: 永斯·雅各布·贝采利乌斯
elements.summary: 钍是一种化学元素，符号为 Th，原子序数为 90。作为一种放射性锕系金属，钍是仅有的两种仍以原生元素大量存在于自然界中的显著放射性元素之一（另一种是铀）。它于 1828 年由瑞典化学家贝采利乌斯鉴定，并以北欧雷神索尔 (Thor) 的名字命名。

image.title: 安瓿瓶中被腐蚀的钍金属
image.attribution: W. Oelen, CC BY-SA 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Thorium is a chemical element with symbol Th and atomic number 90. A radioactive actinide metal, thorium is one of only two significantly radioactive elements that still occur naturally in large quantities as a primordial element (the other being uranium). It was discovered in 1828 by the Norwegian Reverend and amateur mineralogist Morten Thrane Esmark and identified by the Swedish chemist Jöns Jakob Berzelius, who named it after Thor, the Norse god of thunder.

elements.name: 镤
elements.summary: 镤是一种化学元素，符号为 Pa，原子序数为 91。它是一种致密的银灰色金属，极易与氧气、水蒸气和无机酸反应。它形成各种化合物，镤在其中通常以 +5 氧化态存在，但也可以呈现 +4 甚至 +2 或 +3 态。

image.title: 这张镤-233 样品（照片中的深色圆形区域）是在爱达荷州国家反应堆测试站利用其自身的放射性发射光（较亮区域）拍摄的。
image.attribution: ENERGY.GOV, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Protactinium is a chemical element with symbol Pa and atomic number 91. It is a dense, silvery-gray metal which readily reacts with oxygen, water vapor and inorganic acids. It forms various chemical compounds where protactinium is usually present in the oxidation state +5, but can also assume +4 and even +2 or +3 states.

elements.name: 铀
elements.discovered_by: 马丁·海因里希·克拉普罗特
elements.summary: 铀是一种化学元素，符号为 U，原子序数为 92。它是周期表锕系中的一种银白色金属。一个铀原子有 92 个质子和 92 个电子，其中 6 个是价电子。

image.title: 通过埃姆斯工艺还原后的铀金属块。约 1943 年。
image.attribution: Unknown authorUnknown author, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Uranium is a chemical element with symbol U and atomic number 92. It is a silvery-white metal in the actinide series of the periodic table. A uranium atom has 92 protons and 92 electrons, of which 6 are valence electrons.

elements.name: 镎
elements.summary: 镎是一种化学元素，符号为 Np，原子序数为 93。作为一种放射性锕系金属，镎是第一种超铀元素。它在周期表中紧随铀（以天王星命名）之后的位置，使其以海王星（天王星之外的下一颗行星）命名。

image.title: 镎-237 球体（6 公斤）
image.attribution: Los Alamos National Laboratory,, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Neptunium is a chemical element with symbol Np and atomic number 93. A radioactive actinide metal, neptunium is the first transuranic element. Its position in the periodic table just after uranium, named after the planet Uranus, led to it being named after Neptune, the next planet beyond Uranus.

elements.name: 钚
elements.appearance: 银白色，在空气中失去光泽变为深灰色
elements.summary: 钚是一种超铀放射性化学元素，符号为 Pu，原子序数为 94。它是一种外观呈银灰色的锕系金属，暴露在空气中会失去光泽，氧化后形成暗淡的涂层。该元素通常表现出六种同素异形体和四种氧化态。

image.title: 钚环
image.attribution: Los Alamos National Laboratory, Attribution, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Plutonium is a transuranic radioactive chemical element with symbol Pu and atomic number 94. It is an actinide metal of silvery-gray appearance that tarnishes when exposed to air, and forms a dull coating when oxidized. The element normally exhibits six allotropes and four oxidation states.
elements.appearance_en: silvery white, tarnishing to dark gray in air

elements.name: 镅
elements.summary: 镅是一种放射性超铀化学元素，符号为 Am，原子序数为 95。该锕系元素在周期表中位于镧系元素铕之下，因此类比美洲 (Americas) 命名。镅于 1944 年由格伦·T·西博格领导的小组首次合成。

image.title: 显微镜下的镅-241 小圆盘。
image.attribution: Bionerd, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Americium is a radioactive transuranic chemical element with symbol Am and atomic number 95. This member of the actinide series is located in the periodic table under the lanthanide element europium, and thus by analogy was named after the Americas. Americium was first produced in 1944 by the group of Glenn T.Seaborg from Berkeley, California, at the metallurgical laboratory of University of Chicago.

elements.name: 锔
elements.appearance: 银色金属，在黑暗中发出紫色光
elements.summary: 锔是一种超铀放射性化学元素，符号为 Cm，原子序数为 96。该锕系元素以玛丽·居里和皮埃尔·居里的名字命名。锔于 1944 年 7 月由加州大学伯克利分校的格伦·T·西博格小组首次合成并鉴定。

image.title: 一块锔，它发出的强放射性使其产生光芒
image.attribution: European Union, The Actinide Group, Institute for Transuranium Elements (JRC-ITU), source: https://images-of-elements.com/curium.php

elements.summary_en: Curium is a transuranic radioactive chemical element with symbol Cm and atomic number 96. This element of the actinide series was named after Marie and Pierre Curie – both were known for their research on radioactivity. Curium was first intentionally produced and identified in July 1944 by the group of Glenn T. Seaborg at the University of California, Berkeley.
elements.appearance_en: silvery metallic, glows purple in the dark

elements.name: 锫
elements.discovered_by: 劳伦斯伯克利国家实验室
elements.summary: 锫是一种超铀放射性化学元素，符号为 Bk，原子序数为 97。它是锕系和超铀元素系列的成员。它以加利福尼亚州的伯克利市命名，该市是 1949 年 12 月发现该元素的加州大学辐射实验室所在地。

image.title: 花费了 250 天才制造出足够合成 117 号元素的锫（此处显示为溶解状态）
image.attribution: ORNL, Department of Energy, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Berkelium is a transuranic radioactive chemical element with symbol Bk and atomic number 97. It is a member of the actinide and transuranium element series. It is named after the city of Berkeley, California, the location of the University of California Radiation Laboratory where it was discovered in December 1949.

elements.name: 锎
elements.discovered_by: 劳伦斯伯克利国家实验室
elements.summary: 锎是一种放射性金属化学元素，符号为 Cf，原子序数为 98。该元素于 1950 年在伯克利首次通过用 α 粒子轰击锔制得。它是第六个被合成的超铀元素，在所有已生产出肉眼可见数量的元素中，它的原子质量位居第二。

image.title: 一个锎金属盘（249Cf，10 毫克）。该盘的直径约为普通大头针厚度的两倍，即 1 毫米左右。
image.attribution: United States Department of Energy (see File:Einsteinium.jpg), Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Californium is a radioactive metallic chemical element with symbol Cf and atomic number 98. The element was first made in 1950 at the University of California Radiation Laboratory in Berkeley, by bombarding curium with alpha particles (helium-4 ions). It is an actinide element, the sixth transuranium element to be synthesized, and has the second-highest atomic mass of all the elements that have been produced in amounts large enough to see with the unaided eye (after einsteinium).

elements.name: 锿
elements.discovered_by: 劳伦斯伯克利国家实验室
elements.summary: 锿是一种合成元素，符号为 Es，原子序数为 99。它是第七个超铀元素，属于锕系。锿是 1952 年作为第一次氢弹爆炸碎片的成分被发现的，并以阿尔伯特·爱因斯坦的名字命名。

image.title: 300 微克的锿-253，其半衰期为 20 天。
image.attribution: Haire, R. G., US Department of Energy.Touched up by Materialscientist at en.wikipedia., Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Einsteinium is a synthetic element with symbol Es and atomic number 99. It is the seventh transuranic element, and an actinide. Einsteinium was discovered as a component of the debris of the first hydrogen bomb explosion in 1952, and named after Albert Einstein.

elements.name: 镄
elements.discovered_by: 劳伦斯伯克利国家实验室
elements.summary: 镄是一种合成元素，符号为 Fm，原子序数为 100。它是锕系元素的成员。它是通过中子轰击较轻元素所能形成的最重元素，因此也是最后一种可以制备出宏观数量的元素，尽管纯镄金属尚未制得。

image.title: 镄最初是在“艾维·麦克”核试验的放射性尘埃中观察到的。
image.attribution: U.S. Department of Energy, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

elements.summary_en: Fermium is a synthetic element with symbol Fm and atomic number 100. It is a member of the actinide series. It is the heaviest element that can be formed by neutron bombardment of lighter elements, and hence the last element that can be prepared in macroscopic quantities, although pure fermium metal has not yet been prepared.

elements.name: 钔
elements.discovered_by: 劳伦斯伯克利国家实验室
elements.summary: 钔是一种合成元素，化学符号为 Md，原子序数为 101。它是锕系中的一种放射性超铀金属元素，是第一种目前无法通过中子轰击较轻元素来生产宏观数量的元素。

image.title: 这只是插图，不是钔本身。这种放射性极强的重金属在化学上与铥相似，会发出能量极高的 α 辐射。
image.attribution: Chemical Elements A Virtual Museum under a Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported License, source: https://images-of-elements.com/mendelevium.php

elements.summary_en: Mendelevium is a synthetic element with chemical symbol Md (formerly Mv) and atomic number 101. A metallic radioactive transuranic element in the actinide series, it is the first element that currently cannot be produced in macroscopic quantities through neutron bombardment of lighter elements. It is the antepenultimate actinide and the ninth transuranic element.

elements.name: 锘
elements.summary: 锘是一种合成化学元素，符号为 No，原子序数为 102。它以炸药发明者阿尔弗雷德·诺贝尔的名字命名。它是一种放射性金属，是第十个超铀元素，也是锕系倒数第二个成员。

image.title: 这只是插图，不是锘本身。锘只能制造出极少量，并会发出各种强烈的辐射。
image.attribution: Chemical Elements A Virtual Museum under a Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported License, source: https://images-of-elements.com/nobelium.php

elements.summary_en: Nobelium is a synthetic chemical element with symbol No and atomic number 102. It is named in honor of Alfred Nobel, the inventor of dynamite and benefactor of science. A radioactive metal, it is the tenth transuranic element and is the penultimate member of the actinide series.

elements.name: 铹
elements.discovered_by: 劳伦斯伯克利国家实验室
elements.summary: 铹是一种合成化学元素，化学符号为 Lr，原子序数为 103。它以回旋加速器的发明者欧内斯特·劳伦斯的名字命名。铹是一种放射性金属，是第十一个超铀元素，也是锕系系列的最后一个成员。

image.title: 这只是插图，不是铹本身。铹只能制造出极少量，并会发出强烈的辐射。
image.attribution: Chemical Elements A Virtual Museum under a Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported License, source: https://images-of-elements.com/lawrencium.php

elements.summary_en: Lawrencium is a synthetic chemical element with chemical symbol Lr (formerly Lw) and atomic number 103. It is named in honor of Ernest Lawrence, inventor of the cyclotron, a device that was used to discover many artificial radioactive elements. A radioactive metal, lawrencium is the eleventh transuranic element and is also the final member of the actinide series.

elements.name: 𬬻
elements.summary: 𬬻是一种化学元素，符号为 Rf，原子序数为 104，以物理学家欧内斯特·卢瑟福的名字命名。它是一种合成的放射性元素；已知最稳定的同位素 267Rf 的半衰期约为 1.3 小时。在元素周期表中，它是 d 区元素，也是第四行过渡元素的第二个。

image.title: 云室中的衰变轨迹，不是𬬻，而是介子。虽然这是完全不同的粒子，但𬬻的衰变也会产生类似的条纹。
image.attribution: Image © CERN, Chemical Elements A Virtual Museum under a Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported License, source: https://images-of-elements.com/rutherfordium.php

elements.summary_en: Rutherfordium is a chemical element with symbol Rf and atomic number 104, named in honor of physicist Ernest Rutherford. It is a synthetic element (an element that can be created in a laboratory but is not found in nature) and radioactive; the most stable known isotope, 267Rf, has a half-life of approximately 1.3 hours. In the periodic table of the elements, it is a d - block element and the second of the fourth - row transition elements.

elements.name: 𬭊
elements.summary: 𬭊是一种化学元素，符号为 Db，原子序数为 105。它以俄罗斯的小镇杜布纳命名，该元素最早在那里制得。它是一种合成的放射性元素；已知最稳定的同位素𬭊-268 的半衰期约为 28 小时。

image.title: 未找到图片
image.attribution: Chemical Elements A Virtual Museum under a Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported License, source: https://images-of-elements.com/dubnium.php

elements.summary_en: Dubnium is a chemical element with symbol Db and atomic number 105. It is named after the town of Dubna in Russia (north of Moscow), where it was first produced. It is a synthetic element (an element that can be created in a laboratory but is not found in nature) and radioactive; the most stable known isotope, dubnium-268, has a half-life of approximately 28 hours.

elements.name: 𬭳
elements.discovered_by: 劳伦斯伯克利国家实验室
elements.summary: 𬭳是一种合成元素，符号为 Sg，原子序数为 106。其最稳定的同位素 271Sg 的半衰期为 1.9 分钟。最近发现的同位素 269Sg 可能具有稍长的半衰期。

image.title: 未找到图片
image.attribution: Chemical Elements A Virtual Museum under a Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported License, source: https://images-of-elements.com/seaborgium.php

elements.summary_en: Seaborgium is a synthetic element with symbol Sg and atomic number 106. Its most stable isotope 271Sg has a half-life of 1.9 minutes. A more recently discovered isotope 269Sg has a potentially slightly longer half-life (ca.

elements.name: 𬭛
elements.summary: 𬭛是一种化学元素，符号为 Bh，原子序数为 107。它以丹麦物理学家尼尔斯·玻尔的名字命名。它是一种合成的放射性元素；已知最稳定的同位素 270Bh 的半衰期约为 61 秒。

image.title: 未找到图片
image.attribution: Chemical Elements A Virtual Museum under a Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported License, source: https://images-of-elements.com/bohrium.php

elements.summary_en: Bohrium is a chemical element with symbol Bh and atomic number 107. It is named after Danish physicist Niels Bohr. It is a synthetic element (an element that can be created in a laboratory but is not found in nature) and radioactive; the most stable known isotope, 270Bh, has a half-life of approximately 61 seconds.

elements.name: 𬭶
elements.summary: 𬭶是一种化学元素，符号为 Hs，原子序数为 108，以德国黑森州命名。它是一种合成的放射性元素；已知最稳定的同位素 269Hs 的半衰期约为 9.7 秒。迄今为止已合成了 100 多个𬭶原子。

image.title: 未找到图片
image.attribution: Chemical Elements A Virtual Museum under a Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported License, source: https://images-of-elements.com/hassium.php

elements.summary_en: Hassium is a chemical element with symbol Hs and atomic number 108, named after the German state of Hesse. It is a synthetic element (an element that can be created in a laboratory but is not found in nature) and radioactive; the most stable known isotope, 269Hs, has a half-life of approximately 9.7 seconds, although an unconfirmed metastable state, 277mHs, may have a longer half-life of about 130 seconds. More than 100 atoms of hassium have been synthesized to date.

elements.name: 鿏
elements.summary: 鿏是一种化学元素，符号为 Mt，原子序数为 109。它是一种放射性极强的合成元素。已知最稳定的同位素鿏-278 的半衰期为 7.6 秒。

image.title: 未找到图片
image.attribution: Chemical Elements A Virtual Museum under a Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported License, source: https://images-of-elements.com/meitnerium.php

elements.summary_en: Meitnerium is a chemical element with symbol Mt and atomic number 109. It is an extremely radioactive synthetic element (an element not found in nature that can be created in a laboratory). The most stable known isotope, meitnerium-278, has a half-life of 7.6 seconds.

elements.name: 𬭚
elements.summary: 𬭚是一种化学元素，符号为 Ds，原子序数为 110。它是一种放射性极强的合成元素。已知最稳定的同位素𬭚-281 的半衰期约为 10 秒。

image.title: 未找到图片
image.attribution: Chemical Elements A Virtual Museum under a Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported License, source: https://images-of-elements.com/darmstadtium.php

elements.summary_en: Darmstadtium is a chemical element with symbol Ds and atomic number 110. It is an extremely radioactive synthetic element. The most stable known isotope, darmstadtium-281, has a half-life of approximately 10 seconds.

elements.name: 𬬭
elements.summary: 𬬭是一种化学元素，符号为 Rg，原子序数为 111。它是一种放射性极强的合成元素；已知最稳定的同位素𬬭-282 的半衰期为 2.1 分钟。𬬭于 1994 年由德国重离子研究所首次合成。

image.title: 未找到图片
image.attribution: Chemical Elements A Virtual Museum under a Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported License, source: https://images-of-elements.com/roentgenium.php

elements.summary_en: Roentgenium is a chemical element with symbol Rg and atomic number 111. It is an extremely radioactive synthetic element (an element that can be created in a laboratory but is not found in nature); the most stable known isotope, roentgenium-282, has a half-life of 2.1 minutes. Roentgenium was first created in 1994 by the GSI Helmholtz Centre for Heavy Ion Research near Darmstadt, Germany.

elements.name: 鎶
elements.summary: 鎶是一种化学元素，符号为 Cn，原子序数为 112。它是一种放射性极强的合成元素，只能在实验室中制造。已知最稳定的同位素鎶-285 的半衰期约为 29 秒。

image.title: 未找到图片
image.attribution: Chemical Elements A Virtual Museum under a Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported License, source: https://images-of-elements.com/copernicium.php

elements.summary_en: Copernicium is a chemical element with symbol Cn and atomic number 112. It is an extremely radioactive synthetic element that can only be created in a laboratory. The most stable known isotope, copernicium-285, has a half-life of approximately 29 seconds, but it is possible that this copernicium isotope may have a nuclear isomer with a longer half-life, 8.9 min.

elements.name: 鿭
elements.summary: 鿭是一种原子序数为 113 的化学元素，符号为 Nh。它是一种合成元素，具有极强的放射性；其已知最稳定的同位素鿭-286 的半衰期为 20 秒。

image.title: 未找到图片
image.attribution: Chemical Elements A Virtual Museum under a Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported License, source: https://images-of-elements.com/nihonium.php

elements.summary_en: Nihonium is a chemical element with atomic number 113. It has a symbol Nh. It is a synthetic element (an element that can be created in a laboratory but is not found in nature) and is extremely radioactive; its most stable known isotope, nihonium-286, has a half-life of 20 seconds.

elements.name: 𬭟
elements.summary: 𬭟是一种超重人工化学元素，符号为 Fl，原子序数为 114。它是一种放射性极强的合成元素。该元素以俄罗斯杜布纳联合核研究所的弗廖罗夫核反应实验室命名。

image.title: 未找到图片
image.attribution: Chemical Elements A Virtual Museum under a Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported License, source: https://images-of-elements.com/flerovium.php

elements.summary_en: Flerovium is a superheavy artificial chemical element with symbol Fl and atomic number 114. It is an extremely radioactive synthetic element. The element is named after the Flerov Laboratory of Nuclear Reactions of the Joint Institute for Nuclear Research in Dubna, Russia, where the element was discovered in 1998.

elements.name: 镆
elements.summary: 镆是元素周期表中一种合成超重元素的名称，符号为 Mc，原子序数为 115。它是一种极具放射性的元素；其已知最稳定的同位素镆-289 的半衰期仅为 220 毫秒。

image.title: 未找到图片
image.attribution: Chemical Elements A Virtual Museum under a Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported License, source: https://images-of-elements.com/moscovium.php

elements.summary_en: Moscovium is the name of a synthetic superheavy element in the periodic table that has the symbol Mc and has the atomic number 115. It is an extremely radioactive element; its most stable known isotope, moscovium-289, has a half-life of only 220 milliseconds. It is also known as eka-bismuth or simply element 115.

elements.name: 𬭸
elements.summary: 𬭸是一种合成超重元素，符号为 Lv，原子序数为 116。它是一种极具放射性的元素，仅在实验室中制造。该元素以美国的劳伦斯利弗莫尔国家实验室命名。

image.title: 未找到图片
image.attribution: Chemical Elements A Virtual Museum under a Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported License, source: https://images-of-elements.com/livermorium.php

elements.summary_en: Livermorium is a synthetic superheavy element with symbol Lv and atomic number 116. It is an extremely radioactive element that has only been created in the laboratory and has not been observed in nature. The element is named after the Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory in the United States, which collaborated with the Joint Institute for Nuclear Research in Dubna, Russia to discover livermorium in 2000.

elements.name: 鿬
elements.summary: 鿬是一种超重人工化学元素，原子序数为 117，符号为 Ts。它是已知第二重的元素，也是周期表第 7 周期的倒数第二个元素。截至 2016 年，已观察到 15 个鿬原子。

image.title: 未找到图片
image.attribution: Chemical Elements A Virtual Museum under a Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported License, source: https://images-of-elements.com/tenessine.php

elements.summary_en: Tennessine is a superheavy artificial chemical element with an atomic number of 117 and a symbol of Ts. Also known as eka-astatine or element 117, it is the second-heaviest known element and penultimate element of the 7th period of the periodic table. As of 2016, fifteen tennessine atoms have been observed: six when it was first synthesized in 2010, seven in 2012, and two in 2014.

elements.name: 鿫
elements.category: unknown, predicted to be noble gas
elements.summary: 鿫是 IUPAC 对原子序数为 118、元素符号为 Og 的超锕系元素的命名。在元素周期表中，它是一个 p 区元素，也是第 7 周期的最后一个元素。鿫目前是第 18 族中唯一的合成成员。

image.title: 未找到图片
image.attribution: Chemical Elements A Virtual Museum under a Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported License, source: https://images-of-elements.com/oganesson.php

elements.summary_en: Oganesson is IUPAC's name for the transactinide element with the atomic number 118 and element symbol Og. It is also known as eka-radon or element 118, and on the periodic table of the elements it is a p-block element and the last one of the 7th period. Oganesson is currently the only synthetic member of group 18.
elements.category_en: unknown, predicted to be noble gas
